name: Got To Be There: The Apollo, Its People, and Its Stories
description: Step behind the curtain of the world-famous Apollo Theater in this exhibition showcasing the people and stories that shaped the institution's history.
sublocation: The Apollo Stages at The Victoria, Laura and Frank Baker Gallery
short_name: The Apollo, Its People, and Its Stories

name: reMastered: Jac Lahav’s Record Paintings
description: Jac Lahav presents a series of paintings that reinterpret iconic records and musical influences through a visual lens.

name: Joan Jonas: Moving off the Land II (Excerpts)
description: Columbia University School of the Arts presents excerpts from Joan Jonas's multimedia work exploring ocean ecology as part of its 60th anniversary.

name: About a Living Culture
description: A temporary public art installation by Nepalese artist IMAGINE featuring a sculpture inspired by Sanskrit scriptures and Himalayan cultures.

name: CONTEXT: Art, Books, & Freedom
description: This exhibition features 15 artists whose work incorporates books and text, exploring themes of social commentary, political statement, and literary allusion. The artists utilize books and text as tools of investigation, pushing the boundaries of free expression within the context of a liberal arts library.

name: Athi-Patra Ruga: Lord, I gotta keep on (movin’)
description: This exhibition centers on myths, their creation, and their relationship to power and liberation through performance, film, painting, textile, and glass.

name: Soft Spaces
description: An exhibition exploring soft materials and queer narratives, currently on view through mid-January.

name: Feldenkrais Class Returns for 2025 – 2026 classes
description: A Feldenkrais class focusing on movement and comfort. Donations are welcome, and comfortable clothing is recommended.

name: Blanka Amezkua—Power Flower: Curative Flora
description: Explore the vibrant art of Blanka Amezkua in this exhibition focusing on curative flora and healing traditions.

name: *The Amazing Adventures of Kavalier & Clay*
description: A world-premiere opera by Mason Bates based on Michael Chabon’s Pulitzer Prize–winning novel about the golden age of comic books.

name: *Turandot*
description: Franco Zeffirelli’s spectacular production of Puccini’s final opera remains a cornerstone of the Met season.

name: David Wojnarowicz: Arthur Rimbaud in New York
description: A series of photo-performances documenting New York City and the artist’s connection to French poet Arthur Rimbaud during a period of urban decay and personal transition.

name: Family, Favorite Things & Me (Speaking & Listening)
description: This online course for teens introduces basic Japanese language and culture through topics like family and favorite foods. It aims to develop practical listening and speaking abilities in a fun, engaging way.

name: MFTA Gallery: “Manos Que Tejen Luz” by Magda Love (Mondays – Fridays)
description: "Manos Que Tejen Luz" is an immersive tapestry installation by MFTA Artist-in-Residence Magda Love. The colossal work, created with over 250 volunteers, depicts an Edenic landscape and spans the entire gallery space. It transports viewers into a playful garden paradise, celebrating care, nurturing, and belonging.
short_name: “Manos Que Tejen Luz” by Magda Love (Mondays

name: Whose America? Exhibition
description: This exhibition explores diverse perspectives on American identity and experience through various artistic lenses. It showcases works that challenge conventional narratives and invite critical engagement.

name: Open Book(S) (Altered)
description: This exhibition features books that have been creatively altered into sculptures and narrative art pieces.

name: D.S. & Durga Pop-Up
description: Visit this limited-time Brooklyn-based perfumery pop-up to discover modern fragrances and luxury gifts.

name: *La Bohème*
description: Puccini’s timeless story of young love and bohemian life in Paris continues its popular run at the Met.

name: *Carmen*
description: Bizet’s timeless tale of obsession, freedom, and ill-fated passion set in Seville continues its season run.

name: Notice/Know/Kin: A Human/Tree Biography
description: This mixed-media art exhibit by Nina Browne invites visitors inside the artist’s life and inside wood to reflect on our living relationships with trees and the Earth. The works draw on indigenous ecological wisdom to reveal wood as alive and dynamic.

name: Meditation Practice with Steve Clorfeine
description: Join this free weekly meditation practice session. It's a welcoming space for all to find calm and mindfulness.

name: Visualizing Legacies and Futures: An Exhibition by The National Black Theatre
description: This exhibition features a collection of historic archival photography and original film footage from the National Black Theatre that has never been publicly released. It explores the theater's historical and contemporary memory as part of an ongoing residency.
short_name: An Exhibition by The National Black Theatre

name: Legacy Lab: Life Interwoven
description: This exhibition features the works of renowned artists Lola Flash and groana melendez in dialogue with Bronx community archives.

name: Jeff Koons: Porcelain Series
description: This is the first dedicated exhibition of Jeff Koons's Porcelain series, featuring mirror-polished stainless steel sculptures and new paintings. The works explore themes of beauty and visual culture through figurines modeled on eighteenth- to early twentieth-century porcelain.

name: Santa's Secret
description: An immersive holiday speakeasy for ages 21+ featuring a variety show, burlesque, live music, and festive cocktails.

name: Holiday Train Show
description: Experience New York City’s favorite holiday tradition featuring model trains traveling through nearly 200 plant-based landmark replicas. The exhibition includes miniature masterpieces crafted from palm fronds, pinecones, and cinnamon sticks under the glass of the historic conservatory.

name: Evergreen Express
description: Children can explore a treetop walk and take on the role of a train conductor with large outdoor toys. The program includes seasonal crafts like making mini evergreen swags and cozy reading time in the herbarium.

name: Passport to Santa
description: A magical family adventure featuring enchanted holiday worlds, games, character meet-and-greets, and photo sessions with Santa.

name: What If They Ate The Baby?
description: A provocative and boundary-pushing theatrical exploration of societal norms.
short_name: What If They Ate The Baby?

name: Westbeth Winter Show 2025Video, Images, Participants
description: The annual Westbeth Winter Show features recent work by resident artists across various media. It's a celebration of the diverse artistic talent within the community.

name: A Christmas Carol
description: PAC NYC presents an acclaimed production of A Christmas Carol from London's The Old Vic. This immersive staging offers a magical interpretation of Charles Dickens' timeless classic, filled with merriment and holiday cheer.

name: The Nutcracker Afternoon Tea
description: An afternoon tea inspired by the New York City Ballet featuring themed pastries and savory treats.

name: Bronx Community Board 8 Meetings
description: Bronx Community Board 8 meetings are held via Zoom. Details on how to join can be found on the website.

name: December '25 - Committees Agendas
description: This link provides access to the agendas for the committee meetings in December 2025. It's a resource for understanding the topics to be discussed.

name: Art in Focus
description: Rockefeller Center presents a series of public art pop-ups inspired by the New York City landscape and contemporary life. These installations can be found in unique and unexpected locations across the Center.

name: Giving Tuesday-Support Gibney
description: This month, we're celebrating YOU! The artists, movers, and community members who make Gibney come alive every day. Together, Gibney's community reaches over 100,000 individuals each year using dance as a force for creativity, connection, and change. As we launch into this giving season, help us shape what's next. Your support keeps our stages alive with new voices, our studios accessible, and our programs reaching every corner of New York City.

name: *Porgy and Bess*
description: The Gershwins’ beloved American folk opera returns to the Met with its powerful score and moving story of community and resilience.

name: Rockefeller Center Christmas Tree
description: The Rockefeller Center Christmas Tree will be on view nightly from its lighting on December 3rd until mid-January 2026. It is a central attraction for holiday celebrations in New York City.

name: Migrant Housing: Water as a Medium for Healing
description: An exhibition of work by BFA Interior Design students, alumni, and a collaborative group, exploring water as a medium for healing in migrant housing.
short_name: Water as a Medium for Healing

name: fables for introverts, chapter iii: limbo
description: Recent paintings by Gerald Euhon Sheffield II that explore cultural landscapes, American folklore, and national identity following a research fellowship.

name: CALL TO ARTISTS: 25th Annual Winter Art Show
description: BHS is now accepting submissions for their Winter Art Show, which will be presented both online and in person from February 4 to March 1, 2026. The deadline to submit artwork is January 5, 2026.

name: Winter Haven at Wave Hill
description: A seasonal special event celebrating the winter landscape and providing a peaceful retreat during the holidays.

name: NYC Parks’ 43rd Annual Wreath Interpretations
description: NYC Parks is pleased to announce the 43rd annual Wreath Interpretations exhibition. This festive tradition welcomes the holiday season with nearly 40 wreaths made from unique and unusual materials.
sublocation: Third Floor of the Arsenal Building

name: Merry Mayhem
description: Circus Oz returns with a holiday frenzy of uproarious fun from Down Under, featuring high-energy acrobatics.

name: The Gotham All-Stars
description: A professional showcase featuring comics from various popular television shows and films.

name: BIPOC Climb Night
description: Join us for Black, Indigenous, People of Color Climb Night at LIC to climb and be a part of the community! These evenings are hosted by the BIPOC Climbing Collective.

name: An Unmarried Woman
description: Mazursky’s smash comedic drama stars a breakthrough Jill Clayburgh as a well-to-do Manhattan woman whose snake of a husband (Michael Murphy) leaves her, without warning, for a younger woman after sixteen years of marriage.

name: The New York Sari
description: This exhibition showcases the diverse and evolving tradition of the sari in New York. It explores how this garment has been adapted and worn by different communities in the city.

name: The Gay Harlem Renaissance
description: This exhibition illuminates the vibrant LGBTQ+ contributions to the Harlem Renaissance. It highlights the intersection of art, culture, and identity during this pivotal era.

name: Declaring the Revolution: America’s Printed Path to Independence
description: This exhibition traces the pivotal role of print in shaping the American Revolution. It examines how pamphlets, newspapers, and broadsides fueled the movement for independence.

name: Holiday Express: Toys and Trains from the Jerni Collection
description: This festive exhibition showcases a delightful collection of vintage toys and trains. It offers a nostalgic journey through holiday traditions and childhood memories.
short_name: Toys and Trains from the Jerni Collection

name: Stirring the Melting Pot: Photographs from The New York Historical Collections
description: This exhibition presents a compelling collection of photographs documenting the diverse immigrant experience in New York. It captures the city's evolving identity through the lens of its residents.
short_name: Photographs from The New York Historical Collections

name: The Recordings: Voices from the _Shoah_ Tapes
description: This exhibition features oral histories from Holocaust survivors, preserving their powerful testimonies. It offers a profound and personal look at one of history's most tragic events.

name: Saturday Sensory Hour
description: Every Saturday from 10am - 11am, we dim our lights and quiet our exhibits so that visitors can have an all around less stimulating experience here before it gets crowded.

name: Deep Disco
description: Join us for DEEP DISCO, a night of soulful grooves and electronic beats. Get ready to dance the night away!

name: Poetry and Performance Series
description: Poets, spoken word performers, and vocalists activate Rashid Johnson’s Rotunda Stage in a series of weekly performances.

name: Architecture Tour
description: Join us on the rotunda floor to learn more about the Guggenheim’s iconic architecture. 30 minutes, free with museum admission.

name: Soup & Soul
description: Enjoy a cozy afternoon with delicious soup and soulful music. A perfect way to relax and unwind.

name: La Ternura
description: A comedy following three women and three men as they navigate the peculiar realities of love and relationships on a desert island. The play features hilarious mix-ups and magical misunderstandings.

name: Sanguine: Musical Activations
description: Experience weekly musical activations of Rashid Johnson’s Sanguine, a site-specific installation featuring local performers and special guests.

description: Please arrive no earlier than 6:45PM for the meeting. Space is limited. Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

description: Please arrive no earlier than 6:45PM for the meeting. Space is limited. Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda includes discussion of energy cost legislation and the Open Meetings Law.

name: ladies night
description: A monthly meetup for women, nonbinary individuals, and those who feel at home in women-centered spaces. All skill levels are welcome to climb and connect in a supportive, LGBTQIA+ affirming environment.

name: Blues Jam Session
description: Immerse yourself in the raw emotion and improvisation of a live blues jam session. All musicians are welcome to join!

name: December Exclusive Promotions at Aveda
description: Enjoy special holiday offers including buy 3 get 1 free on travel-sized products and exclusive gift set opportunities.

name: Permits and Licenses Committee Meeting
description: This meeting will focus on reviewing liquor license and cannabis retail license applications. Notices with specific establishments will be finalized on December 2nd.

description: Please arrive no earlier than 6:45PM for the meeting. Space is limited. Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

name: Blue Pig Test – Series Event
description: A recurring comedy event series.

name: Youth & Education Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. A presentation on NYC education policy will be given, along with a discussion of the "Freedom to Read Act."

name: CRUX Meetup
description: A climbing meetup for Trans, Non-binary, and Intersex individuals, fostering empowerment through rock climbing. Rentals are included, and no sign-up is necessary.

name: Queens Community Board 4 Monthly Meeting
location: Please call the office of Community Board 4 to verify meeting dates and locations.
description: Boards meet once each month to address community concerns. A portion of each meeting is reserved for public input.

name: La Breve y Maravillosa Vida de Oscar Wao
description: This play explores the endless human capacity to persevere in the name of love, following Oscar, a young Dominican-American man dealing with a family curse. It's a humorous and poignant performance.
short_name: La Breve y Maravillosa Vida de Oscar Wao

name: Es Devlin: Congregation
description: World-renowned artist Es Devlin’s acclaimed choral installation Congregation returns to PAC NYC. It features large-scale chalk and charcoal portraits of 50 Londoners who have experienced forced displacement, presented as a projection-mapped tiered sculpture.

name: Event
description: This is a placeholder event.

name: Grand Rounds
description: This event features a lecture with a title and speaker to be announced, focusing on medical topics. The lecture will be followed by a Q&A session and can be joined in person or via Zoom.

name: Full Board Meeting
location: Lewis Davis Pavilion at 25 Waterside Plaza
description: This is a hybrid meeting where the public can speak on community issues. In-person attendance is limited, so Zoom is recommended.

name: Weekly ParaCliffHangers Meetup
description: ParaCliffHangers strive to make climbing accessible to all while building community and fostering personal empowerment. Join ParaCliffHangers for weekly meetups every Wednesday night where all levels are welcome.

name: Swarovski Gift with Purchase
description: Receive a complimentary star ornament with any purchase of $300 or more during this limited-time holiday promotion.

description: The agenda includes a presentation on NYC Smoke-Free Brooklyn Initiatives and Programs. Further topics are to be confirmed.

description: Agenda for the meeting can be found here. If you wish to view the meeting virtually, you may watch the livestream on our YouTube channel by clicking here.

name: Health & Environment Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. Topics include Bellevue Hospital's budget priorities and a discussion to prohibit nonessential helicopter flights.

name: Christmas Masses and Concerts
description: We invite you to celebrate the Christmas season with us! Read more about all of our masses, concerts, and more, condensed into one post.

name: *The Magic Flute*—Holiday Presentation
description: A family-friendly, abridged English-language holiday presentation of Mozart’s enchanting fairy-tale opera.

name: TUMI Semi Annual Sale
description: Take advantage of additional markdowns during TUMI's semi-annual sale with up to 40% off select luggage and travel styles.

name: Unik Fridays at Amadeus Nightclub
description: Kick off your weekend with Unik Fridays at Amadeus Nightclub. Enjoy a unique atmosphere with great music and an energetic crowd to start your night off right.

name: Stories Around the Table
description: This is a recorded video of a session titled "Stories Around the Table," filmed on October 28, 2025. It likely features discussions or performances by artists.

name: Saturday Nights @ Harbor Nyc
description: Experience the vibrant Saturday nights at Harbor NYC. Enjoy music and a lively atmosphere at this popular New York City venue.

name: Asian Climbing Tribe
description: These meetups will be facilitated by Asian Climbing Tribe leaders and feature rotating partner vendors every month. Asian Climbing Tribe’s mission is to provide a safe space for members of the Asian community.

name: Latin Night
description: Spice up your Friday night with a vibrant Latin party. Dance to salsa, bachata, reggaeton, and more!

name: 30th Poets House Showcase
description: Celebrate the 30th Poets House Showcase, a literary exhibition and reading series of poetry from 2024 and 2025.

name: DTHRR Exhibit Opening
description: Opening night of a new media art exhibit featuring digital artists Entroplay and Vohu Mana, exploring the concept of dithering.

name: Housing & Homelessness Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda includes discussion of the Faith-Based Affordable Housing Act and a housing survey.

name: Sunday Brunch Vibes
description: Kick off your Sunday with a delightful brunch accompanied by smooth, laid-back tunes. A perfect start to your day.

name: Calling Choreographers Apply Now for WestFest Dance 2026!
description: This is an application period for choreographers to be considered for WestFest Dance 2026.
short_name: Calling Choreographers Apply Now for WestFest Dance 2026!

name: Elsewhere: Cartography of the Dream
description: This exhibition explores themes of mapping, navigation, and subconscious states through visual art.

name: Brooklyn Community Board 7 Meeting
description: This is a monthly meeting for Brooklyn Community Board 7. Details about the agenda will be posted closer to the meeting date.

name: Momix Alice
description: This holiday season, tumble into a dazzling world of illusion and imagination with a breathtaking adventure through Wonderland. The performance is filled with acrobatics, magic, and holiday whimsy.

name: Monthly Board Meeting
description: This is the monthly board meeting for Brooklyn Community Board 18, open to the public and conducted via Webex. Details for joining the meeting are provided, including links and access codes.

name: Full Board Meeting (Public Hearing and Regular Meeting)
description: This is the regular monthly meeting for the Full Board, which includes a public hearing and a regular meeting. Public comment will be accepted.

name: Executive Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda includes the Chair's Report, discussion of the CB6 email policy, and review of upcoming committee agendas.

name: Spark Your Health: Yoga Nidra & Sound Bath
description: Join _Spark Your Health_ for a 75-minute Yoga Nidra & Sound Bath experience designed to guide you towards deep relaxation.

name: Israeli Folk Dance (Open Session)
description: An open session for Israeli Folk Dance.

name: 'Twas the Night Before... by Cirque du Soleil
description: The magic of ‘Twas the Night Before… by Cirque du Soleil is back for a limited run this holiday season at The Theater at MSG, December 4-28. For years, Isabella and her father have read “Twas the Night Before Christmas” together, but this year Isabella feels like she’s outgrown the family tradition. Then the magic of the poem comes to life, and Isabella and her father are unexpectedly separated by a snowstorm that sends them on a fantastical journey.
short_name: 'Twas the Night Before... by Cirque du Soleil

name: Gilded Age Mansions of Fifth Avenue
description: This tour focuses on the design and history of the great mansions of Fifth Avenue. Learn about the owners who constructed them as lavish displays of their wealth and status in Gilded Age New York City. Explore the exteriors of grand residences and hear stories of their designers and owners.
short_name: Gilded Age Mansions of Fifth Avenue

name: Aladdin
description: Discover a whole new world at _Aladdin_ , the hit Broadway musical. This production features unforgettable beauty, magic, comedy, and breathtaking spectacle.

name: Amahl and the Night Visitors
location: Lincoln Center Theater - Mitzi E. Newhouse
description: This holiday season, opera star Joyce DiDonato brings her singular voice to a new production of this beloved family opera. It's the tale of how a simple gift can become a miracle that transforms the world.

name: Beetlejuice
description: BEETLEJUICE… BEETLEJUICE… BEEEE… very excited, because the third time’s the charm! This hilariously demented musical tells the story of Lydia Deetz and a demon with a thing for stripes.

name: Buena Vista Social Club
description: Winner of 5 Tony Awards, _Buena Vista Social Club_ brings the Grammy® Award-winning album to life on Broadway. Step into the heart of Cuba for a captivating tale of big dreams and music.

name: Bug
description: _Bug_ is Tracy Letts' cult classic about an unexpected and intense romance between a lonely waitress and a mysterious drifter. This sexy psychological thriller explores paranoia, delusion, and conspiracy.

name: Chicago
description: A true New York City institution, _Chicago_ has everything that makes Broadway great: a universal tale of fame, fortune, and all that jazz; one show-stopping song after another; and the most astonishing dancing you’ve ever seen.

name: Heathers: The Musical
description: Welcome to Westerberg High, where popularity is a matter of life and death. This high octane, black comedy, rock musical is based on one of the greatest cult films of all-time.

name: Katsura Sunshine's Rakugo
description: _Katsura Sunshine's Rakugo_ is a 75-minute performance of traditional Japanese comic storytelling by Canada's only Western Rakugo Master. Performed with minimal props, Sunshine uses only a fan and a hand towel to deliver a hilarious monologue followed by a traditional story. It's a unique bridge between Eastern and Western humor.

name: Hell's Kitchen
description: _Hell's Kitchen_ is the Tony Award-winning musical from 16-time Grammy Award winner Alicia Keys, inspired by her life, music, and community. It follows Ali, a 17-year-old searching for freedom and passion, as she encounters a musical mentor, her family, and the neighborhood that shapes her. The show features a mix of Keys' classics and new songs, brought to life with exhilarating choreography.

name: & Juliet
description: _& Juliet_ is a hilarious new musical that reimagines the classic story of Juliet, asking what happens if she doesn't end it all over Romeo. The show takes audiences on a fabulous journey with a fresh beginning for Juliet, featuring iconic pop songs like "Since U Been Gone" and "...Baby One More Time."

name: Just In Time
description: Tony Award® winner Jonathan Groff returns to Broadway as Bobby Darin in _Just In Time_, a new musical celebrating the legendary singer's life and hits. The show immerses audiences in an intimate nightclub setting and features iconic songs like "Beyond the Sea" and "Mack the Knife." It's described as "a helluva good time at the theater."

name: Liberation
description: _Liberation_ is a critically acclaimed play set in 1970s Ohio, exploring what it means to be free and to be a woman through the story of Lizzie and her daughter. This powerhouse play delves into inherited legacies, forgotten histories, and ongoing fights for understanding. It's a limited Broadway engagement.

name: Little Bear Ridge Road
description: Laurie Metcalf and Micah Stock star in _Little Bear Ridge Road_, a wry and piercing new play by Samuel D. Hunter. The story centers on a reclusive aunt and her estranged nephew in Idaho who confront their tangled history while selling a crumbling house. It's described as bitingly funny and quietly explosive.

name: MJ
description: _MJ_ is a brand-new Broadway musical celebrating the unique artistry of Michael Jackson. With a book by Lynn Nottage and choreography by Christopher Wheeldon, the show offers a rare look at the creative mind behind the legendary star, going beyond his iconic moves and sound.

name: Little Shop Of Horrors
description: The award-winning revival of _Little Shop of Horrors_ continues its run at the Westside Theatre. This musical tells the story of a man-eating plant and the eccentric characters in Skid Row. It's a darkly comedic tale with a catchy score.

name: Mamma Mia!
description: _Mamma Mia!_ , the worldwide musical phenomenon, returns to Broadway's Winter Garden Theatre for a special limited engagement. This feel-good musical, filled with ABBA's greatest hits, tells the story of a mother, a daughter, and three possible dads. It's a joyful escape that has captivated audiences for 25 years.

name: Masquerade
description: _Masquerade_ is a bold, immersive production of Andrew Lloyd Webber's _The Phantom of the Opera_, directed by Diane Paulus. Audiences journey through the Paris Opera House in intimate groups, experiencing the music, mystery, and seduction. Guests are encouraged to wear masks and formal attire for this unique theatrical event.

name: Maybe Happy Ending
description: Winner of 6 Tony Awards including Best Musical, _Maybe Happy Ending_ is the story of a chance encounter that sparks connection, adventure, and maybe even love. Helmed by Tony Award-winning director Michael Arden, this visually stunning show is called "Astonishing. Joyful, heartbreaking, cutting edge."

name: Oh, Mary!
description: _Oh, Mary!_ is a Tony Award-winning dark comedy about Mary Todd Lincoln in the weeks leading up to her husband's assassination, exploring unrequited yearning, alcoholism, and suppressed desires.

name: Operation Mincemeat: A New Musical
description: _Operation Mincemeat_ is the Tony and Olivier Award-winning true story of the secret mission that won World War II, based on a gamble involving a stolen corpse, a fake love letter, and Ian Fleming.

name: Tours, Free Gallery Tours: A Few of Our Favorite Things
description: Discover your new favorite artworks in an immersive tour of the Brooklyn Museum. Join one of our A.R.T. Guides for a free tour through our galleries.
sublocation: Martha A. and Robert S. Rubin Lobby, 1st Floor
short_name: A Few of Our Favorite Things

name: Tours, Free Pop-Up Talks
description: Maximize your time at the Museum! Join a free, short talk to learn about works throughout the Museum with one of our A.R.T. Guides. Conversations are repeated every 15 minutes.
sublocation: Ask for location details at the Admissions Desk

name: Iconic Views of Central Park Tour
description: Visit the iconic landmarks and landscapes that make Central Park the world’s most famous park. This tour is recommended for first-time visitors or those interested in Central Park’s most famous sites.
short_name: Iconic Views of Central Park Tour

name: Wicked: for Good
description: Elphaba, now demonized as The Wicked Witch of the West, lives in exile while continuing her fight for the freedom of Oz's silenced Animals. Glinda, meanwhile, has become the glamorous symbol of Goodness for all of Oz.

name: Nuremberg
description: The Allies have the task of ensuring the Nazi regime answers for the unveiled horrors of the Holocaust while a US Army psychiatrist is locked in a dramatic psychological duel with former Reichsmarschall Herman Göring.

name: Hamnet
description: From Academy Award® winning writer/director Chloé Zhao, _Hamnet_ tells the powerful story of love and loss that inspired the creation of Shakespeare’s timeless masterpiece, Hamlet.

name: It Was Just An Accident
description: Vahid, an unassuming mechanic, has a chance encounter with Eghbal, a man he strongly suspects to be his former sadistic jailhouse captor.

name: Sentimental Value
description: Sisters Nora and Agnes reunite with their estranged father, the charismatic Gustav. When Nora turns down a role in his comeback film, she discovers he has given it to an eager young Hollywood star.

name: Drunk Romeo & Juliet
description: Five professional actors attempt to perform Romeo & Juliet, but one has had 5 shots of whiskey. Hilarity ensues as the drunk actor tries to stay on script, while the sober actors attempt to keep the show on track.

name: Pradosham
location: Hindu Temple Society Of North America
description: Pradosham is an auspicious time dedicated to Lord Shiva. It is observed on the 13th day of the lunar fortnight.

name: Inuuteq Storch Soon Will Summer Be Over
description: This exhibition showcases the work of Inuuteq Storch. Further details are available on the MoMA PS1 website.
short_name: Inuuteq Storch Soon Will Summer Be Over

name: Vaginal Davis Magnificent Product
description: This fall, MoMA PS1 will present a major exhibition of Vaginal Davis, spanning five decades of her practice as a performer, visual artist, author, filmmaker, musician, educator, self-proclaimed “Blacktress,” and countercultural icon. The presentation spotlights Ms. Davis’s role as an underground trailblazer in culture and queer politics—as well as her uncompromising glamour.

name: Four Dilations
description: _Four Dilations_ presents four artistic positions that counter the dominant ways in which time is kept today. The exhibition’s title references temporal dilation, a physical phenomenon that describes how time can pass at different rates for observers with distinct frames of reference.

name: Gabrielle Goliath Personal Accounts
description: The US premiere of Gabrielle Goliath’s (South African, b. 1983) acclaimed, ongoing video series _Personal Accounts_ (2024–25) responds to the impacts of patriarchal violence in a range of global contexts from Johannesburg to Kyiv. Intimately documenting survivor testimonials, in collaboration with the contributors, the artist has withheld narrative speech within the videos, instead portraying the moments in between participants’ recounts: breaths, sighs, cries, humming, and even laughter. Goliath’s sonic cycles trouble false binaries of the ‘voiced’ and ‘voiceless,’ revealing the enormity of what can be conveyed in moments of perceived silence.

name: Homeroom: LA ESCUELA___ Education as Resistance
description: In their first project in the US, artist-run platform LA ESCUELA___ will initiate a collective learning project in Homeroom this fall charting pedagogies from across Latin America.

name: Ayoung Kim Delivery Dancer Codex
description: Artist Ayoung Kim (Korean, b. 1979) presents the US debut of her _Delivery Dancer_ trilogy, renowned video installations presented for the first time together. Recognized as an artist on the vanguard of digital innovation, Kim uses generative AI, videogame engines, and live-action footage to create narratives that collide geopolitics, synthesize mythologies, and interrogate technologies.

name: New York City Ballet: The Nutcracker
description: The New York City Ballet presents its beloved holiday classic, The Nutcracker. Immerse yourself in the magical world of Clara and the Sugarplum Fairy.
short_name: New York City Ballet: The Nutcracker

name: CHAMPS Comedy Open Mic
description: A comedy open mic night where anyone can sign up to perform. Come for the laughs and stay for the community.

name: Trivia Night!
description: Test your knowledge and compete with friends at this weekly trivia night. Prizes and bragging rights are up for grabs.

name: Business Affairs & Licensing Meeting
description: This is a hybrid meeting with an in-person option. The agenda is still being formed, but will include the Chair's Report and old/new business.

name: Bipoc Meetup

Amidst the backdrop of a near apocalyptic Brooklyn, Gooey embarks on an epic journey. The show examines the difficulty of finding true belonging in a gentrifying New York City.

name: New Edition The New Edition Way Tour

short_name: New Edition The New Edition Way Tour

name: St. Lucia
description: Synth-pop favorites St. Lucia perform two special sets for fans at Brooklyn Steel.

name: Tavern Concerts: Irish Stout
description: Celebrate St. Patrick's season with traditional Irish songs and tunes played on banjo, fiddle, and Irish flute. The band performs new and old favorites in the historic Guyon Tavern.

name: Forever Freestyle
location: Lehman Center For The Performing Arts
description: Celebrating 18 years of freestyle music, this event features a massive lineup including TKA, George LaMond, and Judy Torres. Experience a high-energy night of dance hits hosted by Sal Abbatiello and Speedy.

name: Jai Uttal: Mantra Love & Lullaby
description: A deep sonic restorative experience featuring meditative mantra chanting and a spiritual lullaby by Grammy nominee Jai Uttal. No prior experience is needed for this exploration of the heart.

name: Rhythm of the Dance
description: An exhilarating performance celebrating Irish step dance and traditional music with a modern flair.

name: Moondoggy
description: Brooklyn-based outfit Moondoggy performs their unique blend of alternative and indie rock.

name: Before Broadway & Beyond Benefit Concert
description: A benefit concert supporting arts education and showcasing the next generation of Broadway talent.

name: Artemas
description: Artemas performs live featuring special guest Isabel LaRosa. This show is for ages 16 and over.

name: Charla en la sala: Lectura de un edificio (Spanish language)
description: A Spanish-language tour exploring the architecture and history of J. Pierpont Morgan's original 1906 library building.

name: Filthy Rotten System
description: An activist protest/rock band with roots in the Catholic Worker movement performs a repertoire ranging from Woody Guthrie to Bruce Springsteen. They focus on powerful original and traditional songs of social comment and human rights.

name: Jeff Mills
description: Detroit techno legend Jeff Mills, "The Wizard," explores the intersection of sci-fi and electronic music in a visionary performance.

name: Dance Reflections: Lucinda Childs
description: A special performance of Lucinda Childs' Early Works presented as part of the Dance Reflections Festival by Van Cleef & Arpels.

name: Staten Island Parent Ultimate Summer Program & Activity Expo
description: Discover a wide range of summer programs and activities for parents and children at this comprehensive expo. Find the perfect fit for your family's summer fun.
short_name: Staten Island Parent Ultimate Summer Program & Activity Expo

name: Jeremy Denk, Piano
description: Acclaimed pianist Jeremy Denk presents a solo recital featuring a program of classical and contemporary piano works. Enjoy an afternoon of virtuoso piano performance.

name: Bryce Crawford I Love Jesus U.S. Tour
description: Comedian Bryce Crawford brings his "I Love Jesus" U.S. Tour to The Town Hall. Expect a night of stand-up comedy with a unique perspective.
short_name: Bryce Crawford I Love Jesus U.S. Tour

name: NYC Art Bazaar
description: Explore original work across various mediums including painting, printmaking, ceramics, and photography. Meet local creatives and discover new additions for your home or art collection.

name: Park Pals: Mighty Menhaden
description: Celebrate the "most important fish in the sea" through crafts, sensory bins, and a shoreline walk for families.

name: Spring Cleanup at Canarsie Pier
description: Help clean up the shoreline at Canarsie Pier; tools and gloves are provided for all volunteers.

name: Contemporary Music Festival
description: A showcase of modern and contemporary works performed by rising star soloists as part of the Student Sounds series.

name: Three Italian Tenors
location: Lehman Center For The Performing Arts
description: Tenors Giovanni Maria Palmia, Ugo Tarquini, and Alessandro Fantoni perform beloved Italian songs and arias. This North American tour debut pays tribute to legends like Pavarotti and Bocelli through nostalgic classics.

name: Heart of a Dog
description: A satirical play based on Mikhail Bulgakov’s novel about a stray dog’s transformation into a man. Performed in Russian, this production uses immersive multimedia and bold visuals to explore social contradictions.

name: Decorative Paper Techniques: Paste Paper Beth Sheehan
description: Explore the art of paste paper decoration in this virtual workshop. Learn techniques to create unique and textured decorative papers.

name: Ágora
description: Ágora is a week-long celebration uniting LGBTQ+ artists, women, seniors, and grassroots creators. It provides an inclusive space for performance, exchange, and cultural dialogue.

name: Brendan James “I Got a Song to Sing” Tour
description: Experience the heartfelt, piano-driven songwriting and compelling stories of artist Brendan James on tour.
short_name: Brendan James “I Got a Song to Sing” Tour

name: The Reading Series: Sycamore
description: A reading of a new play by Nick Kaidoo, selected by Branden Jacobs-Jenkins, featuring an open conversation between the playwrights.

name: The Devil Reached Toward the Sky: An Oral History of the Atomic Bomb
description: An exploration into the history of the atomic bomb, its development, deployment, and consequences for global politics.
short_name: An Oral History of the Atomic Bomb

name: The Devil Reached Toward the Sky: An Oral History of the Making and Unleashing of the Atomic Bomb
description: Authors delve into the development and deployment of the atomic bomb and its enduring consequences for global politics.
short_name: An Oral History of the Making and Unleashing of the Atomic Bomb

name: Prompt to Page: Designing Artist Books with Creative Prompts Amber Braverman
description: Transform creative prompts into artist book designs in this virtual workshop. Explore methodologies for developing concept-driven artist books and utilize creative prompts to spark new projects.

name: Riso #4: Riso for Photography Eva Parra
description: Discover how to use Riso printing specifically for photographic applications. This in-person workshop explores techniques for translating photographic imagery through the Riso process.
short_name: Riso #4: Riso for Photography Eva Parra

name: Ambient Music (3/3)
description: Final session experimenting with composition and exploring how music creates environment and organizes space.

name: How My Grandparents Fell in Love, A Musical
description: A new musical about romance and family history produced by the New Jersey Repertory Company.
short_name: How My Grandparents Fell in Love, A Musical

name: Across Continents and Generations: Poetry as Memory and Witness
description: An educational program exploring the role of poetry as a medium for memory and witnessing across different historical generations.

name: Concert | Instruments of Affection: Mozart's Violin in Concert with Sonnambula
description: A unique opportunity to hear music performed on one of Mozart's own violins, brought to NYC from the Mozarteum Foundation.

name: John Moreland + Patterson Hood
description: An intimate evening of storytelling and folk-influenced music from John Moreland and Patterson Hood.

name: 'Against Innocence': Miriam Ticktin in Conversation with Ruth Wilson Gilmore
description: Miriam Ticktin discusses her new book, "Against Innocence," in a critical conversation with Ruth Wilson Gilmore about politics, culture, and society.

name: The Hives
description: The Swedish garage rock icons bring their legendary high-energy stage show and impeccable suits to New York. Expect a fast-paced set of rock and roll anthems and charismatic showmanship.

name: G.B.A.T.P.M.O.A.P.F.T.S.I.T.M.T.T. Stands for Getting Back At The Prime Minister Over A Personal Feud That Started In The Mid-2000's
description: A parody play of V for Vendetta and dystopian movies about domestic terrorists wearing silly masks, written by Jason Weitzman.

name: Concerto Köln
description: A classical concert featuring Shunske Sato and Max Volbers performing bridge-building musical works.

name: Affordable Art Fair NYC Spring 2026
description: A massive art fair featuring contemporary works from local and international galleries at accessible prices.

name: Anja Huwe
description: The former frontwoman of Xmal Deutschland performs her distinctive darkwave and post-punk solo material.

name: Mary Beth Barone: Galaxy Brain
description: Comedian and actor Mary Beth Barone brings her ingeniously self-aware standup to the stage for a special filmed performance.

name: Selected Shorts: A Passport to Ireland
description: Author Colum McCann curates an evening of short stories by leading Irish writers, performed by talented actors.
short_name: Selected Shorts: A Passport to Ireland

name: Baby Butcher Choke For Free
description: Noise theater duo laialeah presents an apparatus of spectacle and sound involving acts of surgery and dinner. The piece explores the heart of the matter through loud noise and intervention.

name: Benjamin Stevenson: Everyone in This Bank is a Thief
description: Benjamin Stevenson presents his latest mystery novel regarding a bank heist at the shop.
short_name: Everyone in This Bank is a Thief

name: Neuroscience Seminar Series: Dr. Hokto Kazama
location: Dominick P. Purpura Department of Neuroscience
description: Dr. Hokto Kazama from the Riken Center for Brain Science presents his research as the featured speaker.

name: Justice for All: 150 Years of Legal Aid in New York
description: Lawyers and historians explore the landmark cases and 150-year history of The Legal Aid Society in New York City.
short_name: 150 Years of Legal Aid in New York

name: Jane Comfort and Company
description: A performance by the renowned dance-theatre company featuring their signature mix of movement and story.

name: Cass McCombs + Band
description: Prolific indie-rock artist Cass McCombs performs with his band, featuring special guest Chris Cohen.

name: Tony Avent: Creating a Plant's Home
description: Plantsman Tony Avent shares insights into garden microclimates, soils, and habitats based on his experience building the Juniper Level Botanic Garden. Discover the complexities of managing one of the world's largest collections of perennials.
short_name: Tony Avent: Creating a Plant's Home

name: Clarice Jensen & Chuck Johnson
description: A collaborative performance of atmospheric and contemporary music featuring cellist Clarice Jensen and Chuck Johnson.

name: Exhibition on Screen: Caravaggio
description: A documentary film exploring the revolutionary art and turbulent life of the Italian master Caravaggio.

name: Tony Trischka In Residence: Earl Jam
description: Tony Trischka is joined by Michael Daves for an evening of high-energy bluegrass and traditional tunes.
short_name: Tony Trischka In Residence: Earl Jam

name: PSNY Virtual Workshop: The Pantoum Keeps the Score
description: A workshop focusing on the Malayan pantoum, a poetic form characterized by repeating lines that circle back. Participants learn to master this structure to create evocative, rhythmic verse.

name: Gary Numan
description: Electronic music pioneer Gary Numan brings his influential synth-pop sounds to the White Eagle Hall stage.

name: Dudamel & David Lang’s the wealth of nations
description: Gustavo Dudamel conducts the world premiere of David Lang’s "the wealth of nations," a New York Philharmonic commission. The performance features soloists Fleur Barron and Davóne Tines with the New York Philharmonic Chorus.
short_name: Dudamel & David Lang’s the wealth of nations

name: Joanna Wang
description: Eclectic singer-songwriter Joanna Wang brings her idiosyncratic and captivating pop performance to the stage.

name: Madame Gandhi
description: A high-energy performance mashing together fragments of electro-pop, trap, Afrobeat, and R&B from an icon of empowerment.

name: Project Hail Mary
description: A science teacher wakes up on a spaceship light years from home with no memory of how he got there.

name: Teksupport: Prospa b2b Josh Baker & Mink
description: A collaborative Teksupport event featuring b2b sets from Prospa and Josh Baker.
short_name: Teksupport: Prospa b2b Josh Baker & Mink

name: Bill Orcutt: Guitar Quartet, “How to Rescue Things” & More
description: Legendary guitarist Bill Orcutt performs with collaborators from his eclectic Palilalia record label.
short_name: Guitar Quartet, “How to Rescue Things” & More

name: Hernán Cattáneo
description: Progressive house master Hernán Cattáneo performs a deep, atmospheric set at The Chocolate Factory.

name: Roddy Lima
description: Roddy Lima brings his infectious house sound to the dancefloor at SILO Brooklyn.

name: Jazz at Lincoln Center: Great American Crooners
description: A tribute to the great American crooners presented by world-class musicians from the Jazz at Lincoln Center ensemble.

name: Moontower
description: LA-based electronic pop trio Moontower brings their high-energy, visual-heavy live show to Brooklyn.

name: *La Traviata*
description: Verdi's masterpiece of love and sacrifice in Paris returns to the stage, starting its spring run on March 20.

name: Visite Guidée: Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (French language)
description: A guided tour in French exploring the museum's significant collection of Mozart manuscripts and musical artifacts.

name: Neuroscience WIP series: Ashley Bispo, Catherina Mobini
location: Dominick P. Purpura Department of Neuroscience
description: The weekly WIP series continues with presentations from Ashley Bispo and Catherina Mobini.

name: Mindchatter
description: Singer and producer Mindchatter performs his unique blend of introspective, poetic lyrics and infectious electronic pop.

name: Street Tree Care with Park Slope Fifth Ave BID
description: Help maintain the street trees in Park Slope by mulching and cleaning tree beds alongside the local Business Improvement District.

name: OUTLINE: Geordie Greep, Julianna Barwick & Mary Lattimore, + more
description: An experimental music showcase featuring an eclectic mix of avant-garde rock and atmospheric instrumental performances.
short_name: Geordie Greep, Julianna Barwick & Mary Lattimore, + more

name: The Len Price 3 plus the Chelsea Curve and the Cynz
description: A high-energy night of 60s Mod sound and garage rock featuring the UK's Len Price 3 along with NYC's The Cynz and The Chelsea Curve.
short_name: The Len Price 3 plus the Chelsea Curve and the Cynz

name: Seagull: True Story
description: A politically charged retelling of Molochnikov’s attempt to stage Chekhov’s play, exploring censorship and artistic survival.

name: Martha Graham Dance Company Preview
description: Martha Graham Dance Company previews a new work by Jamar Roberts ahead of their 100th season.
